zh.lifeSummary: 當John Wesley Sheffeild Jr於1764年出生在美國北卡羅來納州摩爾時，他的父親John Alex Sheffield37歲，母親Hannah Melton30歲。他於1779年與Sarah Diffee在美國北卡羅來納州摩爾結婚。他們至少是8個兒子的父母。他去世於1826年6月2日在美國北卡羅來納州摩爾享年62歲。

nameMeanings.name: Sheffeild
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Sheffield (Yorkshire), but perhaps also from Sheffield Bottom in Burghfield (Berkshire), or Sheffield Park in Fletching (Sussex). The Yorkshire place takes its name from the river Sheaf, itself from Old English scēath ‘boundary’, + Old English feld ‘open country’, while the Berkshire placename derives from Old English scēo ‘shelter’ + feld. The Sussex placename comes from Old English scēap ‘sheep’ + feld.

variants.name: Shuffield
meanings: English: variant of   Sheffield  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Wesley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the surname of the founder of the Methodist Church, John Wesley ( 1703–91 ), and his brother Charles ( 1707–88 ), who was also influential in the movement. Their family must have come originally from one or other of the various places in England called Westley, the ‘western wood, clearing, or meadow’. The given name was at first confined to members of the Methodist Church, but is now widely used without reference to its religious connotations.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.

historicEvents.title: Becomes 12th State
historicEvents.description: On November 21, 1789, North Carolina became the 12th state in the Union.

historicEvents.title: Gold Nuggets Found
historicEvents.description: "In 1799, in Little Meadow Creak located in Cabarrus County, North Carolina a large yellow """"rock"""" was found by Conrad Reed. A few years later it was determined that the """"rock"""" was a gold nugget."
historicEvents.urlTitle: Legends of America- Reed Gold Mine, North Carolina- First Gold Discovery

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: Making States Equal
historicEvents.description: The Missouri Compromise helped provide the entrance of Maine as a free state and Missouri as a slave state into the United States. As part of the compromise, slavery was prohibited north of the 36°30′ parallel, excluding Missouri.

en.lifeSummary: When John Wesley Sheffeild Jr was born in 1764, in Moore, North Carolina, United States, his father, John Alex Sheffield, was 37 and his mother, Hannah Melton, was 30. He married Sarah Diffee in 1779, in Moore, North Carolina, United States. They were the parents of at least 8 sons. He died on 2 June 1826, in Moore, North Carolina, United States, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Wesley Sheffeild Jr est né en 1764, à Moore, Caroline du Nord, États-Unis, son père, John Alex Sheffield, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Hannah Melton, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Sarah Diffee en 1779, à Moore, Caroline du Nord, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils. Il est décédé le 2 juin 1826, à Moore, Caroline du Nord, États-Unis, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Wesley Sheffeild Jr 1764 in Moore, North Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Alex Sheffield 37 und seine Mutter Hannah Melton 30. Er hat 1779 in Moore, North Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten Sarah Diffee geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne. Er starb am 2. Juni 1826 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Moore, North Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten.

ja.lifeSummary: John Wesley Sheffeild Jrが1764年にアメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州ムーアで生まれた時，父John Alex Sheffieldは37歳，母Hannah Meltonは30歳であった。1779年にアメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州ムーアでSarah Diffeeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子の親であった。彼は1826年6月2日にアメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州ムーアで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Wesley Sheffeild Jr(이)가 1764년 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 무어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Alex Sheffield(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Hannah Melton(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1779년 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 무어에서 Sarah Diffee(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1826년 6월 2일 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 무어에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Wesley Sheffeild Jr родился в 1764 г. в местности мур, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени John Alex Sheffield было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Hannah Melton, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Diffee в 1779 г. в местности мур, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей. Он умер 2 июня 1826 г. в местности мур, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 62 лет.
